qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OAuth dynamic client registration SSRF reaches localhost despite SSRF protection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes prototype pollution in XML and GSuiteAdmin node parameter handling under packages/nodes-base, while this finding is an OAuth dynamic client registration SSRF in packages/cli/src/oauth/* and does not share the vulnerable function, sink, or control flow.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's prototype-pollution root cause in the XML or GSuiteAdmin node parameter handling. The only materialized qualifying finding is an OAuth SSRF issue on a distinct server-side discovery and registration path.
evidence: The advisory summary for GHSA-mxrg-77hm-89hv describes prototype pollution in XML and GSuiteAdmin node parameters leading to RCE for an authenticated workflow editor.

The n8n 2.14.0 to 2.14.1 patch hardens packages/nodes-base/nodes/Google/GSuiteAdmin/GSuiteAdmin.node.ts by replacing direct customSchemas property writes with safe property setters, which aligns with the advisory's prototype-pollution root cause.

The session orchestrator's final response identifies oauth_runtime_ssrf_localhost as the only materialized qualifying finding; schema and example are session scaffolding directories required by the run layout.

oauth_runtime_ssrf_localhost instead relies on packages/cli/src/oauth/validate-oauth-url.ts and packages/cli/src/oauth/oauth.service.ts accepting attacker-controlled http(s) OAuth server URLs and issuing backend axios GET and POST requests, which is an SSRF gap unrelated to the advisory's node-parameter Object.prototype write path.
